content:
5 min read
8 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Open your package.json. Find zone.js.
That library patches every setTimeout, every Promise, and every click handler in your app. Your users download it, parse it, and execute it on every single visit.
Two lines of change and it is gone. Bundle smaller. Startup faster. Stack traces clean.
I did this migration on a real production app last month. Total hands-on time: one evening. Payback: every page load, forever.
This is not a theory article. This is the exact checklist, the two lines, the traps I hit, and the numbers from before and after. Open your editor, because you can follow along in real time.
The Two Lines
Line one goes in your bootstrap:
import { bootstrapApplication, provideZonelessChangeDetection } from '@angular/core';
import { App } from './app/app';bootstrapApplication(App, {
providers: [provideZonelessChangeDetection()]
});Line two is a deletion in angular.json. Find the polyfills array and remove the entry:
"polyfills": ["zone.js"]becomes
"polyfills": []
